text: No Man's Sky might not be the greatest game ever, but it will still take 5 hours from my day in the blink of an eye. I think it might be creating a time paradox, and could be considered dangerous.

lots of dog and monster and fighting is fun and might make you cry the bit with the echo flower that says thats my wish too made me cry a little but im really tired. i love pagetti and thank you mr fox
i love kim, must have in your steam library

After hitting 100% completed on this game I can say in my opinion overall its a 5/10 game. 

The games start and mid game was very fun building up your tavern and managing the customers and stock levels was very enjoyable. Along with some other features that had potential (such as the adventurer table and brewery) but just didn't expand on the very basic setup it had. 

After around 7 hours of playing though the game pretty much just an afk simulator and after 100% this game I have no real interest in playing though a second time. In the end I ended up skipping the days to get though the very tedious quest system to progress though the tracker. Overall though its probably still worth the price if its on a discount. Took 15 hours to 100%.

Update: I have now played the game again after its last patch. This is what it should have been at launch, it runs smooth, plays well and now there's a pause button. The game has great atmosphere and game-play. Its such a shame this is not what we got, and it ruined the devs. 

This game and Arkane deserved better. Besides the crazy amount of miss-able achievements this game is pretty good and worth getting.  Its fun solo and I would love to play coop, but sadly those days have passed. 


Original review: I have tried to get into it a few times. I actually enjoy the gameplay and the setting. I like the designs and ascetics. But this game plays very poorly, also some of the gameplay choices, such as non replayable missions and a huge amount of missable achievements, suck.

I'd ask for a refund but I got it for free with my video card upgrade.

I'm still debating about asking nVidia for a refund. Not worth the time or money, and not even a good way to show off my 4090.
